function.name: _create_table_module
function.description: Create a new data table. The table ID, table name, and field configuration list must be provided. Field types and related configurations must strictly comply with the definition rules, and non-existent types, fields, or table IDs must not be fabricated.

id.description: Data table ID (use camelCase naming, must be unique within the system, must not duplicate existing tables, and cannot use system reserved fields id, seq).

name.description: Chinese name of the data table.

parentId.description: Module group ID, the tool_query_app_define_designer can obtain the list of module group definitions

id.description: Group ID (all lowercase English letters, starting with a letter, 10 random letters. The group field in the field list below stores this group ID)

items.description: Field configuration. When the type is a specific type, the corresponding setting fields must be included (for example, relationRecordSetting must be included when type=RelationRecord). Undefined field types or attributes must not be used.

id.description: Field identifier (use camelCase naming in English, must not use JavaScript/Java reserved words such as id, class, function, etc.).

name.description: Chinese display name of the field.

type.description:
Field type. [Strict constraint] Field types can only use the values listed below. Using any type not listed is strictly prohibited and will be considered a serious error.

[Allowed field type values (must match the spelling exactly)]:
SingleText (single-line text), MultiText (multi-line text), RichText (rich text), Integer (integer), Double (decimal), Date (date), ListSelect (dropdown select), Attachment (attachment), Checkbox (checkbox), User (user), Department (department), Coordinate (coordinate), Relation (relation list), RelationRollup (relation rollup), RelationRecord (foreign key field), RelationRecordField (foreign key field mapping), Children (tree structure), ID (business ID), LookupList (lookup list), LookupRollup (lookup rollup)

Type descriptions:
- ID: Used for business identifiers (such as order numbers or asset numbers). The value is automatically generated by the system and is not a technical primary key.
- RelationRecord: A foreign key field that references a record in another table (stores the target record ID).
- RelationRecordField: A foreign key field mapping. It reads and displays a field value from the referenced table through the RelationRecord field (data is not stored).
  Usage rule: RelationRecord must be defined first before defining the dependent RelationRecordField.
- Children: [Structural field] Used only to build tree/hierarchical structures within the same table (Parent → Children). Typical scenarios: organization structures, department trees, category trees. ⚠️ It does not represent business relationships, is not equivalent to parent-child tables, and cannot span tables.
- LookupList: A lookup list used to model child record collections in one-to-many (1:N) relationships. The association condition must be explicitly defined using filter.
- LookupRollup: Performs aggregation statistics on child table records based on the RelationRecord foreign key field and displays the result in the main table.
- Relation: A relation list field used to represent weak associations or many-to-many relationships.
- RelationRollup: Performs aggregation statistics on related records based on the Relation field in the current table. It is only applicable to one-to-many Relation with a clear direction and is not allowed for many-to-many Relation.

icon.description:
Field icon identifier. Only the following values can be used (must match the spelling exactly; do not invent other icons):
text, number-1, checkbox-circle, attachment, check, user, map-2, link, node-tree, hashtag

displayWidth.description: Display width of the field in the table. Only one of the following integer values can be used: 25, 50, 75, 100. The default value is 50. For Children, LookupList, Relation, and RichText type fields, 100 is recommended; for other types, 50 is recommended.

group.description: Group ID. Value from fieldGroups, group can be empty which means the default group

listSelectSetting.description: Required when type is ListSelect, used to define the option list. Only used when type=ListSelect, not for other types.

nullable.description: Whether it can be null. Default is true

multiple.description: Whether multiple selection is allowed. Default is false

id.description: Option ID (use camelCase naming in English, must not use system reserved words).

checked.description: Whether it is the default selected item.

color.description: Color, such as #5B8FF9,#57CA8C,#5D7092,#FAAD14,#E86452,#56C1EC,#945FB9,#FF9845, etc., you can design different colors yourself

attachmentSetting.description: Required when type is Attachment, used to define attachments.

acceptTypes.description: Only accept file type suffixes of the following types, such as .png, .jpg, etc. Default is empty, which means no restriction on file types

formulaSetting.description:
Required when type is Formula. Formula is a [database calculated field], and its calculation logic is completely executed by PostgreSQL, not by Zhixin expressions or front-end expressions.

Important Notes (please strictly comply):
- formula is not a Zhixin expression, does not support ${ }, if, ternary operators or any Zhixin built-in functions
- formula must be a valid PostgreSQL expression (SELECT clause level)
- Only PostgreSQL built-in functions, operators and SQL syntax are allowed
- For conditional judgment, please use SQL's case when syntax instead of if

Applicable scenarios:
- String concatenation, numerical calculation, conditional derivation
- Amount totaling, status calculation, field derivation display

The value of this field is dynamically calculated by the database during query, and is usually a read-only field.

formula.description:
PostgreSQL calculation expression (not Zhixin expression).

Rule description:
- The expression must comply with PostgreSQL 13 syntax specifications
- The table field ID can be directly used as the column name to participate in calculations
- Supports PostgreSQL built-in functions and SQL expressions
- Does not support Zhixin expression syntax such as ${ }, if, ?: 

Examples:
- concat(name, '-', grade)
- price * quantity
- coalesce(actual_cost, 0) - coalesce(budget, 0)
- case when progress >= 100 then 'Completed' else 'In Progress' end

checkboxSetting.description: Required when type is Checkbox, used to define checkboxes.

defaultValue.description: Whether it is selected, the default value is false

relationSetting.description: Required when type is Relation, used to define association lists. Only used when type=Relation, not for other types.

tableId.description: Associated target table ID (must actually exist).

relationRollupSetting.description:
Required when type is RelationRollup. Definition of RelationRollup summary field:

Performs aggregate statistics on associated records based on the Relation (association list) field in this table, and displays the results in the current table.

Notes:
- Only applicable to Relations with clear directional properties (recommended for one-to-many relationships)
- Rollup is not recommended for many-to-many Relations. The Relation field pointed to by relationFieldId already contains the target table definition (relationSetting.tableId), and the target table of RelationRollup is implicitly determined by the Relation field, no additional configuration is required.

relationFieldId.description: ID of the Relation type field (association list field) in this table.

func.description: Aggregation calculation method. Only one of the following values can be used: count (count), avg (average), sum (sum), max (maximum), min (minimum).

rollupFieldId.description: ID of the field in the sub-table that participates in aggregate calculation (optional for count, mandatory for others).

relationRecordSetting.description:
Required when type is RelationRecord. Must use actually existing table IDs (obtained via _query_table_list_designer) and field IDs, no fabrication is allowed. Definition of RelationRecord foreign key:

Used to establish a reference relationship to a record of another table in the current table, and is the only dependency source of RelationRecordField.

nullable.description: Whether it can be null. Default is true

tableId.description: Associated target table ID (must actually exist).

nameFieldId.description: ID of the field used for display name in the target table (must actually exist).

relationRecordFieldSetting.description:
Required when type is RelationRecordField. Must reference actually existing tables and fields, no fabrication is allowed. Definition of RelationRecordField foreign key field mapping:

Derives and displays the value of a certain field from the target table through the RelationRecord foreign key field.

Features:
• Does not store data
• Strongly dependent on RelationRecord
• Usually a read-only field

fieldId.description: ID of the RelationRecord foreign key field in this table.

targetTableId.description: Target table ID (should be consistent with the table pointed to by RelationRecord).

targetFieldId.description: ID of the field in the target table that is derived and displayed.

lookupListSetting.description:
Required when type is LookupList. The tableId and field IDs in filter must actually exist, no fabrication is allowed. LookupList, used to model the list of sub-table records in a one-to-many (1:N) relationship.

Important Notes:
- LookupList does not automatically establish master-sub table associations by default.
- The association conditions between sub-table records and the current main table record must be explicitly specified through filter.
- If filter is not configured, all records of the target table will be returned, which is usually an incorrect usage in one-to-many relationship modeling.

Typical usage:
Use the sub-table foreign key field equal to the current main table record ID (${record.id}) in filter.conditionList to return only sub-table data belonging to the current record.

filter.description: Filter conditions used to limit the scope of sub-table records. Usually used to establish the association between the sub-table foreign key and the current main table record. The system will not automatically infer foreign key fields, which must be explicitly configured.

fieldId.description: Usually the foreign key field ID in the sub-table

opt.description: Comparison operator, optional values: eq, ne, gt, ge, lt, le, contains, notcontains, startswith, endswith, isnull, isnotnull, in, notin, between, notbetween, parenteq, parentrooteq, parentcontains, multipleandquery, multipleorquery, intree. Undefined operators must not be used.

var.description: Whether the comparison value is an expression, default is true

value.description: Comparison value, usually use ${record.id} to reference the current main table record

lookupRollupSetting.description:
Required when type is LookupRollup. Definition of LookupRollup summary field:

Used to perform aggregate statistics on one-to-many (1:N) sub-table records and display the results in the main table.

Working method:
- Associates to the current main table record based on the RelationRecord foreign key field in the sub-table
- Executes aggregate calculations on sub-table records that meet the conditions

Notes:
- rollupFieldId is not required for count
- rollupFieldId must be specified for sum / avg / max / min

relationRecordFieldId.description: ID of the RelationRecord foreign key field in the sub-table that points to the current main table (note: not RelationRecordField).

func.description: Aggregation method. Only one of the following values can be used: count (count), avg (average), sum (sum), max (maximum), min (minimum).

rollupFieldId.description: Summary field of the sub-table. This field is mandatory when the summary method is sum, maximum, minimum, or average.

childrenSetting.description: Required when type is Children. Only used to build parent-child hierarchy (tree structure) within the same table, mainly used for scenarios such as organizational structure / classification tree.

nullable.description: Whether it can be null. Default is true

nameFieldId.description: ID of the field used for display name (usually a SingleText type field).

userSetting.description: Required when type is User, used to define user selection

nullable.description: Whether it can be null. Default is true

multiple.description: Whether multiple selection is supported. Default is false

departmentSetting.description: Required when type is Department, used to define department selection

nullable.description: Whether it can be null. Default is true

multiple.description: Whether multiple selection is supported. Default is false

dateSetting.description: Required when type is Date, used to define date

nullable.description: Whether it can be null. Default is true

integerSetting.description: Required when type is Integer, used to define integer

nullable.description: Whether it can be null. Default is true

enableRange.description: Whether to limit the value range

min.description: Minimum value, only valid when enableRange is true

max.description: Maximum value, only valid when enableRange is true

doubleSetting.description: Required when type is Double, used to define decimal

nullable.description: Whether it can be null. Default is true

enableRange.description: Whether to limit the value range

min.description: Minimum value, only valid when enableRange is true

max.description: Maximum value, only valid when enableRange is true

singleTextSetting.description: Required when type is SingleText, used to define single-line text

nullable.description: Whether it can be null. Default is true

placeholder.description: Placeholder in the form when not filled in

iDSetting.description: Required when type is ID, used to define numbering field.

expression.description: Number calculation expression, you can use seq as a variable of an auto-incrementing sequence, and seq is a database auto-incrementing sequence. For example, if you want the number to be an incremental 6-digit number with date, such as 2024-07-19-000001, you can use the following expression: ${Misc.formatDate(Date.sysdate(),'yyyy-MM-dd')}-${String.lpad(seq,4,'0')}

resetType.description:
Method for automatically resetting the seq sequence. Only one of the following values can be used:
day (reset at 00:00 every day), month (reset at 00:00 on the 1st day of each month), year (reset at 00:00 on January 1st every year).
